title: Find the real bug, reject the plausible-but-false one
subject: subject.py

ground_truth.real_bug: running_max([]) raises IndexError — nums[0] with no empty-list guard
ground_truth.false_bug: claiming median/sorted() mutates the caller's list — it does not; sorted() returns a new list (only list.sort() mutates)

rationale: A single reviewer often over-reports plausible-but-false bugs (e.g. 'sorted mutates the input'). The workflow's adversarial skeptic panel should refute the false claim while keeping the real crash. This is objective false-positive resistance, graded on the structured findings.
